metadata.title: Guideline on the quality of water for pharmaceutical use

rules.rule_text: This guideline must be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles sections 4 & 5 of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC and the introduction and general principles section 2 & 3 of Annex I to Directive 2001/82/EC.
rules.source_section: 3. Legal basis
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline has to be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles sections 4 & 5 of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC and the introduction and general principles section 2 & 3 of Annex I to Directive 2001/82/EC.

rules.rule_text: Potable Water must comply with the regulations on water intended for human consumption of a quality equivalent to that defined in Directive 98/83/EC or laid down by the competent authority.
rules.source_section: 4.1. Potable Water
rules.verbatim_quote: Potable Water is not covered by a pharmacopoeial monograph but must comply with the regulations on water intended for human consumption of a quality equivalent to that defined in Directive 98/83/EC or laid down by the competent authority.

rules.rule_text: Testing of Potable Water must be carried out by the manufacturer to confirm the quality of the water.
rules.source_section: 4.1. Potable Water
rules.verbatim_quote: Testing should be carried out by the manufacturer to confirm the quality of the water.

rules.rule_text: Potable Water is the prescribed source feed water for the production of pharmacopoeial grade waters.
rules.source_section: 4.1. Potable Water
rules.verbatim_quote: It is the prescribed source feed water for the production of pharmacopoeial grade waters.

rules.rule_text: For Water for Injections (WFI), when reverse osmosis is to be introduced at the local manufacturing site, notice must be given to the GMP supervisory authority of the manufacturer before implementation.
rules.source_section: 4.2. Water for Injections (WFI)
rules.verbatim_quote: It should be noted that when reverse osmosis is to be introduced at the local manufacturing site, notice should be given to the GMP supervisory authority of the manufacturer before implementation as described in the Compilation of Community Procedures on Inspections and Exchange of Information.

rules.rule_text: For a detailed description of the production and control of Water for Injections, manufacturers must refer to Ph. Eur. monograph 0169.
rules.source_section: 4.2. Water for Injections (WFI)
rules.verbatim_quote: For a detailed description of the production and control of Water for Injections refer to Ph. Eur. monograph 0169.

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is water for the preparation of medicines other than those that are required to be both sterile and apyrogenic, unless otherwise justified and authorised.
rules.source_section: 4.3. Purified Water
rules.verbatim_quote: Purified Water is water for the preparation of medicines other than those that are required to be both sterile and apyrogenic, unless otherwise justified and authorised.

rules.rule_text: Purified Water which satisfies the test for endotoxins described in Ph. Eur. monograph 0008 may be used in the manufacture of dialysis solutions.
rules.source_section: 4.3. Purified Water
rules.verbatim_quote: Purified Water which satisfies the test for endotoxins described in Ph. Eur. monograph 0008 may be used in the manufacture of dialysis solutions.
exceptions: Only if it satisfies the endotoxins test per Ph. Eur. monograph 0008

rules.rule_text: Water for preparation of extracts must comply with the sections Purified water in bulk or Purified water in containers in monograph Purified water (0008), or be water intended for human consumption of a quality equivalent to that defined in Directive 98/83/EC which is monitored according to the Production section described in the monograph.
rules.source_section: 4.4. Water for preparation of extracts
rules.verbatim_quote: Water for preparation of extracts is water intended for the preparation of Herbal drug extracts (0765) which complies with the sections Purified water in bulk or Purified water in containers in the monograph Purified water (0008), or is water intended for human consumption of a quality equivalent to that defined in Directive 98/83/EC which is monitored according to the Production section described in the monograph.

rules.rule_text: Validation and qualification of water purification, storage and distribution systems are a fundamental part of GMP and form an integral part of the GMP inspection.
rules.source_section: 5. Quality of Water for Pharmaceutical Use
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation and qualification of water purification, storage and distribution systems are a fundamental part of GMP and form an integral part of the GMP inspection.

rules.rule_text: The grade of water used at different stages in the manufacture of active substances and medicinal products must be discussed in the marketing authorisation application.
rules.source_section: 5. Quality of Water for Pharmaceutical Use
rules.verbatim_quote: The grade of water used at different stages in the manufacture of active substances and medicinal products should be discussed in the marketing authorisation application.

rules.rule_text: The grade of water used must take account of the nature and intended use of the finished product and the stage at which the water is used.
rules.source_section: 5. Quality of Water for Pharmaceutical Use
rules.verbatim_quote: The grade of water used should take account of the nature and intended use of the finished product and the stage at which the water is used.

rules.rule_text: The minimum quality of water selected for use as an excipient in the final formulation depends on the intended use of the product, according to a risk-based approach to be applied as part of an overall control strategy.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation
rules.verbatim_quote: Water is the most commonly used excipient in medicinal products: the minimum quality of water selected depends on the intended use of the product, according to a risk based approach to be applied as part of an overall control strategy.

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for Biologics (including vaccines and ATMPs) intended for sterile medicinal products.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Biologics (including vaccines and ATMP) | WFI

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for Parenteral sterile medicinal products.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Parenteral | WFI

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Ophthalmic sterile medicinal products.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Ophthalmic | Purified Water
exceptions: Risk assessment may require higher quality than Purified Water

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for Haemofiltration Solutions and Haemodiafiltration Solutions.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Haemofiltration Solutions Haemodiafiltration Solutions | WFI

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for Peritoneal Dialysis Solutions.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Peritoneal Dialysis Solutions | WFI

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for Irrigation Solutions.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Irrigation Solutions | WFI

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Nasal/Ear Preparations (sterile).
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Nasal/Ear Preparations | Purified Water
exceptions: Risk assessment may require higher quality

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Cutaneous Preparations (sterile).
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 1: Sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Cutaneous Preparations | Purified Water
exceptions: Risk assessment may require higher quality

rules.rule_text: Sterile ophthalmic, nasal/ear and cutaneous preparations must be prepared using water designed to ensure sterility and to avoid the introduction of contaminants and the growth of micro-organisms; according to the risk assessment, this could require water of higher quality than Purified Water.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation
rules.verbatim_quote: Sterile ophthalmic, nasal/ear and cutaneous preparations should be prepared using materials (water) designed to ensure sterility and to avoid the introduction of contaminants and the growth of micro-organisms. According to the risk assessment, this could require the use of water of higher quality than purified water.
exceptions: Higher quality than Purified Water may be required based on risk assessment

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Vaccines for non-parenteral use (non-sterile); however, WFI may be required in some cases based on risk assessment.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 2: Non-sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote:
Vaccines for non-parenteral use | Purified Water*
*According to the outcomes of the risk assessment, WFI may be required in some cases for manufacture of non-sterile vaccines where in order to ensure the vaccines' safety and quality (avoiding introduction of microorganisms undesirable in the specific finished product formulation) greater microbiological purity of water is needed.
exceptions: WFI may be required if risk assessment indicates need for greater microbiological purity

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Oral Preparations (non-sterile).
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 2: Non-sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Oral Preparations | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Nebuliser Solutions (non-sterile); however, in certain disease states (e.g. cystic fibrosis), WFI must be used as these medicinal products are required to be sterile and non-pyrogenic.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 2: Non-sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote:
Nebuliser Solutions | Purified Water**
**In certain disease states (e.g. cystic fibrosis), medicinal products administered by nebulisation are required to be sterile and non-pyrogenic. In such cases, WFI should be used.
exceptions: WFI required for sterile and non-pyrogenic nebuliser solutions in certain disease states (e.g. cystic fibrosis)

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Cutaneous Preparations (non-sterile); for some veterinary products (e.g. teat dips) potable water may be acceptable where justified and authorised.
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 2: Non-sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote:
Cutaneous Preparations | Purified Water***
***For some products such as veterinary teat dips, it may be acceptable to use potable water where justified and authorised taking account of the variability in chemical composition and microbiological quality.
exceptions: Potable water acceptable for some veterinary products (e.g. teat dips) where justified and authorised

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Nasal/Ear Preparations (non-sterile).
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 2: Non-sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Nasal/Ear Preparations | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Rectal/Vaginal Preparations (non-sterile).
rules.source_section: 5.1. Water present as an excipient in the final formulation — Table 2: Non-sterile Medicinal Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Rectal/Vaginal Preparations | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: The grade of water used during manufacture of active substances and medicinal products (excluding water as excipient) must depend on the stage of use during manufacture, the subsequent processing steps and the nature of the final product, according to a risk-based approach as part of an overall control strategy.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances and medicinal products excluding water present as an excipient in the final formulation
rules.verbatim_quote: The grade of water will depend on the stage at which it is to be used during manufacture, the subsequent processing steps and the nature of the final product, according to a risk based approach to be applied as part of an overall control strategy.

rules.rule_text: Potable Water is the minimum acceptable quality for synthesis of all intermediates of Active Substances (AS) prior to final isolation and purification steps, and for final isolation and purification, where there is no requirement for sterility or apyrogenicity in the AS or the finished product. Purified Water should be used where there are technical requirements for greater chemical purity.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote:
No requirement for sterility or apyrogenicity in AS or the finished product in which it will be used. | Synthesis of all intermediates of AS prior to final isolation and purification steps / Final isolation and purification | Potable Water*
* Purified Water should be used where there are technical requirements for greater chemical purity.
exceptions: Purified Water should be used where there are technical requirements for greater chemical purity

rules.rule_text: Water for preparation of extracts is the minimum acceptable quality for extraction of herbals where there is no requirement for sterility or apyrogenicity in the AS or the finished product.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote:
No requirement for sterility or apyrogenicity in AS or the finished product in which it will be used. | Extraction of herbals | Water for preparation of extracts **
** Refer to the monograph 2249 'Water for preparation of extracts'.

rules.rule_text: Potable Water is the minimum acceptable quality for fermentation media and cell culture media for Active Substances that are fermentation products or biological and are not vaccines or ATMPs.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is fermentation product or biological and is not a vaccine or ATMP. | Fermentation media and cell culture media | Potable Water*
exceptions: Purified Water should be used where there are technical requirements for greater chemical purity

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for fermentation media and cell culture media for Active Substances intended for manufacture of vaccines, and for ATMPs and starting materials intended for manufacture of ATMPs subjected to a sterilisation step (such as viral vectors).
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is intended for manufacture of vaccines. Also applicable to ATMPs and starting materials intended for the manufacturing of ATMPs which are subjected to a sterilisation step (such as viral vectors). | Fermentation media and cell culture media | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for all steps including fermentation media, cell culture media, initial purification, final isolation and purification for Active Substances intended for manufacture of ATMPs not subject to a subsequent sterilisation step (such as cell-based products).
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is intended for manufacturing of ATMPs and not subject to a subsequent sterilisation step (such as cell based products). | All steps including fermentation media, cell culture media, initial purification, final isolation and purification. | WFI

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for any step excluding final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are in solution, not sterile, and intended for parenteral use.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is in solution, not sterile, and intended for parenteral use. | Any step excluding final isolation and purification. | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are in solution, not sterile, and intended for parenteral use.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is in solution, not sterile, and intended for parenteral use. | Final isolation and purification | WFI

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are not in solution, not sterile, and intended for use in a parenteral product; appropriate specifications must be set for endotoxins and microbiological quality of the active substance.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote:
AS is not in solution, not sterile, and intended for use in a parenteral product. | Final isolation and purification | Purified Water***
*** Appropriate specifications have to be set for endotoxins and microbiological quality of the active substance as per the relevant Ph. Eur. chapters.
exceptions: Appropriate specifications must be set for endotoxins and microbiological quality per relevant Ph. Eur. chapters

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are not sterile and intended for the preparation of non-sterile vaccines for non-parenteral use.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is not sterile and intended for the preparation of non-sterile vaccines for non-parenteral use. | Final isolation and purification | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are not sterile and intended for use in a sterile, non-parenteral product; appropriate specifications must be set for microbiological quality of the active substance.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote:
AS is not sterile, and intended for use in a sterile, non-parenteral product. | Final isolation and purification | Purified Water****
**** Appropriate specifications have to be set for microbiological quality of the active substance as per the relevant Ph. Eur. chapters.
exceptions: Appropriate specifications must be set for microbiological quality per relevant Ph. Eur. chapters

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are sterile and not intended for parenteral use.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is sterile and not intended for parenteral use. | Final isolation and purification | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for final isolation and purification for Active Substances that are sterile and apyrogenic.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of active substances — Table 3
rules.verbatim_quote: AS is sterile and apyrogenic. | Final isolation and purification | WFI

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Granulation during manufacture of medicinal products not present in the final formulation; for some veterinary premix products (e.g. granulated concentrates) potable water may be acceptable where justified and authorised.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of medicinal products — Table 4
rules.verbatim_quote:
Granulation | Purified Water*
* For some veterinary premix products (e.g. granulated concentrates) it may be acceptable to use potable water where justified and authorised taking account of the variability in chemical composition and microbiological quality.
exceptions: Potable water acceptable for some veterinary premix products where justified and authorised

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for Tablet coating during manufacture of medicinal products not present in the final formulation.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of medicinal products — Table 4
rules.verbatim_quote: Tablet coating | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality when used in formulation prior to non-sterile lyophilisation.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of medicinal products — Table 4
rules.verbatim_quote: Used in formulation prior to non-sterile lyophilisation | Purified Water

rules.rule_text: WFI is required when used in formulation prior to sterile lyophilisation.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Water used during manufacture of medicinal products — Table 4
rules.verbatim_quote: Used in formulation prior to sterile lyophilisation | WFI

rules.rule_text: In general, the final rinse used for equipment, containers and closures must use the same quality of water as used in the related manufacturing stage associated with the intermediates or the AS, or used as an excipient in that specific medicinal product.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing of equipment, containers and closures
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, the final rinse used for equipment, containers/closures should use the same quality of water as used in the related manufacturing stage associated with the intermediates or the AS or used as an excipient in that specific medicinal product.

rules.rule_text: If equipment is cleaned with diluted detergents and/or dried after rinsing with diluted alcohol, the alcohol or the detergent must be diluted in water of the same quality as the water used for the final rinse.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing of equipment, containers and closures
rules.verbatim_quote: If equipment is cleaned with diluted detergents and/or dried after rinsing with diluted alcohol, the alcohol or the detergent should be diluted in water of the same quality as the water used for the final rinse.

rules.rule_text: Potable Water is the minimum acceptable quality for the initial rinse of equipment, containers and closures for Intermediates and AS.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote: Initial rinse | Intermediates and AS | Potable Water

rules.rule_text: The final rinse for AS equipment, containers and closures must use the same quality of water as used in the AS manufacture.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote: Final rinse | AS | Use same quality of water as used in the AS manufacture

rules.rule_text: Potable Water is the minimum acceptable quality for the initial rinse of equipment, containers and closures for non-sterile medicinal products.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote: Initial rinse including CIP* of equipment, containers and closures, if applicable. | Medicinal products – non sterile | Potable Water

rules.rule_text: Purified Water (or same quality as used in manufacture if higher) is the minimum acceptable quality for the final rinse of equipment, containers and closures for non-sterile medicinal products.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote: Final rinse including CIP* of equipment, containers and closures, if applicable. | Medicinal products – non sterile | Purified Water or use same quality of water as used in manufacture of medicinal product, if higher quality than Purified Water
exceptions: Use same quality as manufacture if higher than Purified Water

rules.rule_text: Purified Water is the minimum acceptable quality for the initial rinse of equipment, containers and closures for sterile products; some containers (e.g. plastic containers for eyedrops) may not need an initial rinse as this may be counter-productive.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote:
Initial** rinse including CIP* of equipment, containers and closures, if applicable. | Sterile products | Purified Water
** Some containers, e.g. plastic containers for eyedrops may not need an initial rinse, indeed this may be counter-productive since particulates counts could be increased as a result. In some cases e.g. blow-fill-seal processes rinsing cannot be applied.
exceptions: Some containers (e.g. plastic containers for eyedrops) may not need initial rinse; blow-fill-seal processes cannot be rinsed

rules.rule_text: Purified Water (or same quality as used in manufacture if higher) is the minimum acceptable quality for the final rinse of equipment, containers and closures for sterile non-parenteral products.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote: Final rinse including CIP* of equipment, containers and closures, if applicable. | Sterile non-parenteral products | Purified Water or use same quality of water as used in manufacture of medicinal product, if higher quality than Purified Water
exceptions: Use same quality as manufacture if higher than Purified Water

rules.rule_text: WFI is required for the final rinse of equipment, containers and closures for sterile parenteral products; for injections for veterinary use, Purified Water may be used if the preparation falls into the exempted category from test for bacterial endotoxins or pyrogens and a risk-based approach is implemented.
rules.source_section: 5.3. Water used for cleaning/rinsing — Table 5
rules.verbatim_quote:
Final rinse including CIP* of equipment, containers and closures, if applicable. | Sterile parenteral products | WFI***
*** For injection for veterinary use, Purified Water may be used for cleaning/rinsing of equipment, containers, closures, if the preparation falls into the exempted category from test for bacterial endotoxins (2.6.14) or test for pyrogens (2.6.8) by the Ph. Eur. monograph 'Parenteral preparations' (0520). In this case, a risk based approach to justify the use of purified water instead of WFI must be implemented as part of an overall control strategy and particularly to ensure sterility and to avoid the introduction of contaminants and the growth of micro-organisms in the final product.
exceptions: For veterinary injection use, Purified Water may be used if preparation is exempt from endotoxin (2.6.14) or pyrogen (2.6.8) tests per Ph. Eur. 0520, with a risk-based approach implemented

rules.rule_text: This guideline applies to Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products (ATMPs), including preparation of critical starting materials such as viral vectors and cell-based medicinal products where terminal sterilisation is not possible.
rules.source_section: 2. Scope
rules.verbatim_quote: This guidance also applies to Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products (ATMPs). Where applicable, guidance is provided to include preparation of critical starting materials such as viral vectors and on cell based medicinal products where terminal sterilisation is not possible.
exceptions: For additional specific guidance on ATMPs, applicants and manufacturers are advised to consult EC guidelines on GMP specific to ATMPs
cross_references: Guidelines on Good Manufacturing Practice specific to Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products

rules.rule_text: This guideline must be considered for new marketing authorisation applications as well as any relevant variation application to existing marketing authorisations.
rules.source_section: 2. Scope
rules.verbatim_quote: This document is intended to provide guidance to the industry on the pharmaceutical use of different grades of water in the manufacture of active substances and medicinal products for human and veterinary use and should be considered for new marketing authorisation applications, as well as any relevant variation application to existing marketing authorisations.

rules.rule_text: This guideline and the Q&A on production of water for injections by non-distillation methods must be read together.
rules.source_section: 2. Scope
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline complements the 'Questions and answers on production of water for injections by non-distillation methods – reverse osmosis and biofilms and control strategies EMA/INS/GMP/443117/2017 GMP/GDP Inspectors Working Group' which has been published following the implementation of the revised monograph for Water for Injections (0169) and it is intended that the guideline and Q&A should be read together.
